exampleSentences: Kết quả học tập của anh năm nay khá kém so với năm trước.
Dịch vụ ở nhà hàng này được nhiều người đánh giá là kém.
Sức khỏe của bà dạo này yếu và thị lực cũng kém hơn trước.
notes_md:
**kém** means *poor, weak, not good* in quality or ability. It’s used to describe performance, skills, health, quality, etc.

It’s an adjective, so it comes after the noun or after “thì/là” in a sentence. You can also use degree words like **rất, khá, hơi** before it.

It often shows comparison: **kém hơn** = worse than / not as good as.  

For ability, it can mean “bad at” something. Strong opposites: **giỏi** (good at), **tốt** (good), **mạnh** (strong).

definitions.vi: Không tốt; thiếu chất lượng hoặc khả năng.
definitions.es: No bueno; falta de calidad o habilidad.
definitions.en: Not good; lacking in quality or ability.

exampleSentencesTranslated: His academic results this year are quite poor compared to last year.
The service at this restaurant is rated as poor by many people.
Her health has been weak lately and her eyesight is also poor compared to before.
notes:
kém means poor, weak, not good in quality or ability. It’s used to describe performance, skills, health, quality, etc.

It’s an adjective, so it comes after the noun or after “thì/là” in a sentence. You can also use degree words like rất, khá, hơi before it.

It often shows comparison: kém hơn = worse than / not as good as.  

For ability, it can mean “bad at” something. Strong opposites: giỏi (good at), tốt (good), mạnh (strong).

translation.en: I do this exercise worse than that one.

content.sentence: Tôi làm bài này ___ hơn bài kia.
